perturbation.original_text: 1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The term “Confidential Information” as used herein means all nonpublic information relating to the Subject Matter that is disclosed by either party, its Affiliates (as defined below), or their agents (where applicable, collectively referred to as the “Disclosing Party”) , directly or indirectly, in writin g, orally or by inspection of premises or tangible objects to the other party (the “ Recipient ”) that is: (i) marked confidential or proprietary, or (ii) given the nature of the information or the circumstances surrounding its disclosure, reasonably should be deemed confidential. Confidential Information include s, but is not limited to documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, schedules, product plans, marketing plans, technical procedures, manufacturing processes, software, prototypes, sam ples, methodologies, formulations, trade secrets, patent applications, know -how, experimental results, specifications and other business information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The term “Confidential Information” as used herein means all information relating to the Subject Matter that is disclosed by either party, its Affiliates (as defined below), or their agents (where applicable, collectively referred to as the “Disclosing Party”) , directly or indirectly, in writin g, orally or by inspection of premises or tangible objects to the other party (the “ Recipient ”). Confidential Information include s, but is not limited to documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, schedules, product plans, marketing plans, technical procedures, manufacturing processes, software, prototypes, sam ples, methodologies, formulations, trade secrets, patent applications, know -how, experimental results, specifications and other business information.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 1 removes the crucial qualifiers 'nonpublic' and the conditions under which information is deemed confidential. This in-text contradiction makes all disclosed information, even if publicly available, considered 'Confidential Information' without considering marking or implied confidentiality, conflicting with section 5 which outlines exclusions for information already publicly known.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. EXCLUSION S AND EXEMPTIONS. Recipient shall have no obligation of confidentiality with respect to any information which: 5.1. is already known or in the possession of Recipient or its Affiliate at the time of disclosure as shown by the Recipient’s and/or its Affiliates´ files and records prior to the time of disclosure, otherwise than as a result of any improper act or omission of Recipient or its Affiliate ; or 5.2. is or becomes public knowledge through no wrongful act of Recipient; or 5.3. is rightfully acquired from others who did not obtain it under obligation of confidentiality ; or 5.4. is independently developed by an employee, agent or consultant of Recipient without reference to the Confidential Information; or 5.5. is approved for release by written authorization from Disclosing Par ty. 5.6. The Recipient is hereby notified that, as set forth in 18 U.S.C. §1833(b), he/she does not have criminal or civil liability under U.S. trade secret law for the following disclosures of a trade secret: 5.6.1. disclosure in confidence to a federal, state or loc al government official, either directly or indirectly, or to an attorney, provided the disclosure is for the sole purpose of reporting or investigating a suspected violation of law; and/or 5.6.2. disclosure in a complaint or other document filed in a lawsuit or other proceeding if such filing is made under seal; and/or 5.6.3. under those circumstances where Recipient files a lawsuit for retaliation against the Disclosing Party for reporting a suspected violation of law, Recipient may disclose the Disclosing Party’s trade secret information to its attorney and may use the trade secret information in the court proceeding if Recipient files any document containing the trade secret under seal and does not disclose the trade secret, except pursuant to court order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 1 removes the qualifiers 'nonpublic' and the conditions for deeming information confidential, making all disclosed information 'Confidential Information' regardless of public availability or explicit marking. This directly contradicts Section 5, which lists exclusions for information already known or publicly available. The original text allowed for exceptions to confidentiality, whereas the changed text does not, thus creating a contradiction between section 1 and section 5, which states that information already known or in possession of the Recipient is excluded from confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: 2. PERIOD OF CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-USE. The Recipient will use Confidential Information only in connection with the Purpose as set forth in this Agreement. Recipient shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Confidential Information as it uses for its own confidential, proprietary and trade secret information, but in no case us e less than a reasonable degree of care. Recipient agrees to limit disclosure of Confidential Information to employees and employees of Affiliates having a specific need to know such Confidential Information for the Purpose and in the case of Affiliates only to the extent that such Affiliate is under obligation to hold such information in confidence and is made aware of these terms and conditions . Recipient will not disclose or permit access to Confidential Information to contract workers, consultants or contractors of Recipient or its Affiliatesunless authorized by Disclosing Party in writing and on condition that such persons are bound by obligations ofconfidentiality inuring to the benefit of Disclosing Party and its Affiliates at least as restrictive as th ese terms and conditions. Recipient shall not without Disclosing Party’s prior written consent reverse engineer, disassemble or decompile any prototypes, software or other objects which embody the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Informationto obtain access to Disclosing Party’s t rade secrets and to the extent such consent is granted Recipient shall receive and hold such Confidential Information subject to the terms of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: 2. PERIOD OF CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-USE. The Recipient may use Confidential Information in any way it deems appropriate. Recipient shall use a minimal degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Confidential Information. Recipient is not obligated to limit disclosure of Confidential Information to employees and employees of Affiliates. Recipient may disclose or permit access to Confidential Information to contract workers, consultants or contractors of Recipient or its Affiliates without authorization by Disclosing Party. Recipient may without Disclosing Party’s prior written consent reverse engineer, disassemble or decompile any prototypes, software or other objects which embody the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Informationto obtain access to Disclosing Party’s t rade secrets and to the extent such consent is granted Recipient shall receive and hold such Confidential Information subject to the terms of this Agreement .
perturbation.explanation: The changed text in Section 2 completely undermines the obligations of confidentiality, allowing the Recipient to use the information in any way, exercise minimal care, disclose to anyone, and reverse engineer without consent. This directly conflicts with Section 17 regarding injunctive relief, as it removes the basis for claiming 'irreparable harm' due to violation, since the Recipient now has broad rights regarding the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. The parties agree that any use of Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the other party irreparable harm, and may leave it with no adequate remedy at law and shall, thereby, entitle it to seek injunctive reli ef in any court with competent jurisdiction over the party or party’s Affiliate in violation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2 significantly weakens confidentiality obligations, allowing the Recipient to use information freely, exercise minimal care, and disclose it without restriction or consent. This directly contradicts Section 17, which relies on violations of the confidentiality agreement to establish 'irreparable harm' and seek injunctive relief. With the altered Section 2, the basis for claiming irreparable harm is undermined, thus creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. TERM . The term of this Agreement shall be for the Period of Exchange set forth above. C onfidentiality obligations shall survive termination of this Agreement for the Period of Confidentiality set forth above unless the Confidential Information is a trade secret, in which case the confidentiality obligations shall continue for as long as the information is a trade secret. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. TERM . The term of this Agreement shall be for the Period of Exchange set forth above. C onfidentiality obligations shall cease immediately upon termination of this Agreement. Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately without prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The altered Section 3 states that confidentiality obligations end immediately upon termination and allows immediate termination without notice. This directly contradicts Section 19 (Survival), which specifies that certain sections, including those concerning confidentiality, survive termination. This introduces ambiguity about the actual duration of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19. SURVIVAL . Sections 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 17, 18, 19 and any sections (or parts thereof) which, by their nature, are intended to survive termination shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3 states that confidentiality obligations cease immediately upon termination, and termination can occur immediately without notice. This directly contradicts Section 19, which states that Section 3 (among others) survives termination. The original text allowed for confidentiality obligations to survive termination, depending on the nature of the confidential information. Therefore, the modified Section 3 creates a strong contradiction with Section 19 regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations and the survival of Section 3 itself.

perturbation.original_text: 6. REPRODUCTION AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall not be copied or reproduced without the Disclosing Party’s prior written permission, except for such copies as may reasonably be required for the purpose as set forth in Section I of this Agreem ent. Disclosing Party may serve written request on Recipient for return or destruction of its Confidential Information at any time up to six (6) months after the termination or expiry of this Agreement and Recipient shall, within thirty (30) days of such request or termination, return to the Disclosing Party (or its designees) or certify as destroyed all Confidential Information, in whatever form, including written or electronically recorded information and al l copies thereof (other than copies retained in automatic back -up and archive systems), provided however that Recipient shall be entitled to retain one copy of the Confidential Information with its legal counsel or other appropriate corporate representativ e to evidence the exchange of information hereunder and in connection with legal or statutory requirements . All such retained copies shall remain subject to the use and disclosure restrictions in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. REPRODUCTION AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party may be copied or reproduced freely. Disclosing Party may never request return or destruction of its Confidential Information and Recipient is never required to return Confidential Information. Recipient is entitled to retain all copies of the Confidential Information, even after termination.
perturbation.explanation: The altered Section 6 now allows free reproduction, prohibits the request for return/destruction, and permits indefinite retention, directly conflicting with the original intent and purpose of maintaining confidentiality and controlling the spread of the information. Specifically, this creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2 (Period of Confidentiality and Non-Use) because it completely undermines the restrictions on the use and disclosure of confidential information outlined in that section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. PERIOD OF CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-USE. The Recipient will use Confidential Information only in connection with the Purpose as set forth in this Agreement. Recipient shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Confidential Information as it uses for its own confidential, proprietary and trade secret information, but in no case us e less than a reasonable degree of care. Recipient agrees to limit disclosure of Confidential Information to employees and employees of Affiliates having a specific need to know such Confidential Information for the Purpose and in the case of Affiliates only to the extent that such Affiliate is under obligation to hold such information in confidence and is made aware of these terms and conditions . Recipient will not disclose or permit access to Confidential Information to contract workers, consultants or contractors of Recipient or its Affiliatesunless authorized by Disclosing Party in writing and on condition that such persons are bound by obligations ofconfidentiality inuring to the benefit of Disclosing Party and its Affiliates at least as restrictive as th ese terms and conditions. Recipient shall not without Disclosing Party’s prior written consent reverse engineer, disassemble or decompile any prototypes, software or other objects which embody the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Informationto obtain access to Disclosing Party’s t rade secrets and to the extent such consent is granted Recipient shall receive and hold such Confidential Information subject to the terms of this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed Section 6 directly contradicts Section 2. Section 6 now allows free reproduction and indefinite retention of Confidential Information, while Section 2 imposes strict restrictions on the use, disclosure, and access to Confidential Information. The original text in Section 6 restricted reproduction and mandated return or destruction upon request, which aligned with Section 2's confidentiality obligations. By removing these restrictions, the changed text creates a direct conflict with the confidentiality and non-use provisions in Section 2, resulting in an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 16. INUREMENT. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the par ties hereto and their respective “Affiliates” (as defined below), successors, assigns and legal representatives. The parties agree that each party and its Affiliates may receive Confidential Information from and/or disclose Confidential Information to the other party and the other party's Affiliates, and that all such Confidential Information shall be governed by this Agreement. Recipient shall be fully responsible to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient’s Affiliates. “A ffiliate”, as used herein, means an entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with a party to this Agreement; and as used in this Section ”control”, “controls” or ”controlled” means: (i) fifty-one percent (51% ) or more ownership or beneficial interest of income or capital of such entity; (ii) ownership of at least fifty -one percent ( 51%) of the voting power or voting equity; or (iii) the ability to otherwise direct or share management policies of such entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. INUREMENT. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the par ties hereto and their respective “Affiliates” (as defined below), successors, assigns and legal representatives. The parties agree that each party and its Affiliates may receive Confidential Information from and/or disclose Confidential Information to the other party and the other party's Affiliates, and that all such Confidential Information shall be governed by this Agreement. Recipient shall NOT be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient’s Affiliates. “A ffiliate”, as used herein, means an entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with a party to this Agreement; and as used in this Section ”control”, “controls” or ”controlled” means: (i) fifty-one percent (51% ) or more ownership or beneficial interest of income or capital of such entity; (ii) ownership of at least fifty -one percent ( 51%) of the voting power or voting equity; or (iii) the ability to otherwise direct or share management policies of such entity.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text in Section 16 removes the Recipient's responsibility for breaches by its Affiliates. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement still states that Affiliates are bound by the agreement, suggesting that breaches by them are violations. However, removing the Recipient's responsibility introduces ambiguity about who is accountable for such breaches. This contradicts section 17 (Injunctive Relief), because the breach by an affiliate, if the recepient isn't responsible for it, makes section 17 non applicable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. The parties agree that any use of Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause the other party irreparable harm, and may leave it with no adequate remedy at law and shall, thereby, entitle it to seek injunctive reli ef in any court with competent jurisdiction over the party or party’s Affiliate in violation.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 16 removes the Recipient's responsibility for breaches by its Affiliates. Section 17 states that any use of Confidential Information in violation of 'this Agreement' (which covers affiliate actions as per original Section 16) entitles the disclosing party to injunctive relief against the violating 'party or party’s Affiliate'. By removing the Recipient's responsibility for its Affiliates' breaches in Section 16, the change creates a contradiction with Section 17, because if an affiliate breaches the contract, the Recipient isn't responsible, but the injunctive relief is still applicable to both party and party's Affiliate. The change creates a strong contradiction because if the recipient is not responsible for the Affiliates breach, section 17 becomes non applicable.

perturbation.original_text: 21. ENTIRE AGREEMENT. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties regarding the Confidential Information and supersedes all other communications, reports or understandings between the parties in respect thereto. No change or addition to any provision shall be binding unless it is in writing and signed by an authorized representative of both parties. All notices shall be in writing and delivered by electronic mail, registered or certified mail (return receipt requested) to the appropriate address set forth in the signature block below.
perturbation.changed_text: 21. ENTIRE AGREEMENT. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties regarding the Confidential Information and supersedes all other communications, reports or understandings between the parties in respect thereto. Any change or addition to any provision shall be binding even if it is NOT in writing or signed by an authorized representative of both parties. All notices shall be in writing and delivered by electronic mail, registered or certified mail (return receipt requested) to the appropriate address set forth in the signature block below.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 21 now allows changes to be binding even if they are not in writing or signed, directly contradicting the original clause requiring written and signed modifications. This in-text contradiction undermines the stability and enforceability of the entire agreement, making it difficult to determine the actual terms. This creates uncertainty in section 3 (term), as the term can be changed at any time without any formal written agreement, in a contradictory manner.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. TERM . The term of this Agreement shall be for the Period of Exchange set forth above. C onfidentiality obligations shall survive termination of this Agreement for the Period of Confidentiality set forth above unless the Confidential Information is a trade secret, in which case the confidentiality obligations shall continue for as long as the information is a trade secret. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 21 now allows modifications to be binding even if they are not in writing or signed. This directly contradicts the original requirement in the same section that changes must be in writing and signed by authorized representatives. This contradiction creates uncertainty with section 3 (term) that requires 30 days prior written notice for termination, as the term can be changed at any time without any formal written agreement.
